The classification of Aphelandra ornata reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Acanthaceae family.

intro: Aphelandra ornata, also known as the Zebra Plant, is a tropical flowering plant native to Central and South America, characterized by its striking zebra-like striped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Aphelandra ornata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
